nameMeanings.name: d'Andeville
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel, Andre, Armand, Gabrielle, Camille, Emile, Fernand, Fernande, Gaston, Jean-Paul, Lucien, Michel.

French: habitational name from Mandeville, the name of two places in Normandy, derived from Latin magna villa ‘large estate’, or a variant of Manneville, a habitational name from Manneville, the name of several places in Normandy (see 2 below). Compare Montville and Monville .
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Manneville (Seine-Maritime, Calvados), Manneville-sur-Risle (Eure), or Magneville (Manche).

variants.name: Mansfield
meanings: English: habitational name from a place so named in Nottinghamshire. The placename means ‘open land by the river Maun’, from the river name Maun (which may take its name from a hill named with Old English mamme ‘teat’) + Old English feld ‘open country’.
Irish (Waterford): when not an importation of 1, this is an altered form of the Norman name de Manville (see Mandeville 3).
Americanized form of German and Jewish (Ashkenazic) Mansfeld, a habitational name from a place so called in Saxony.

variants.name: Manderville
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Mandeville  .

variants.name: Manwell
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Mandeville  .

variants.name: Manville
meanings: English (Sussex and Lancashire): variant of   Mandeville  .
Altered form of   Mainville   1, a surname of Swiss French origin (see   Miville  ).
French: variant of Menville, a habitational name from Menville in Haute-Garonne, and also of Mainville 2.

variants.name: Mundell
meanings: Scottish (of Norman origin): variant of   Mandeville  .
Americanized form of German Mündel (or some other similar, like-sounding surname): nickname for someone with a prominent mouth, from Middle High German munt, mündelīn ‘(little) mouth’.

variants.name: Monville
meanings: Altered form of French   Mandeville  .
Walloon: habitational name from Monville in Namur province in Wallonia (Belgium), of the same etymology as in 3 below.
In some cases possibly also French: habitational name from Montville in Seine-Maritime or Monville in Calvados, both named with Old French mont ‘mountain, hill’ + ville ‘settlement’.

variants.name: Mandal
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Chandi, Sanjay, Sankar, Somnath, Aditi, Aditya, Ajit, Amala, Amitabha, Anil, Anirudh, Aparna.
Indian (northern states): from Sanskrit maṇḍala, a word that has several meanings, including ‘sphere’ and ‘halo’.
English (Cumbria; of Norman origin): variant of   Mandeville  .

variants.name: Montville
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Emile, Vernice.
Altered form of French   Mandeville  .
French: variant of   Monville  . This surname is very rare in France, found mainly in Creuse.

variants.name: Manfull
meanings: English (Midlands): probably a variant of   Mandeville  . Compare   Manville  .

en.lifeSummary: Mrs. d'Andeville was born about 1188, in Stratford on Avon, Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom. She had at least 1 son with Ralph d'Andeville. She died in 1211, at the age of 24.
